title: Coelogyne papillitepala
taxonomy: Coelogyne papillitepala is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Coelogyne within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is part of the family Orchidaceae. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. This classification helps to organize and understand the complex relationships between different plant species.

intro: Coelogyne papillitepala, also known as the Coelogyne orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne papillitepala include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne papillitepala is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in the form of capsules or tablets for its medicinal benefits.
